schema.note: This file is read and edited exclusively by LLMs. All fields documented here are always present on every entry of the matching kind — null when unknown or not yet applicable. Never omit a field that belongs to a kind's shape. Some fields are only non-null in specific statuses — see status_semantics for which fields apply to which statuses.

universal_fields.id: string — F### for fix, D### for design, P### for perf. Zero-padded to three digits. ID prefix reflects kind at time of filing; if kind is reclassified, the ID prefix does not change (reclassification is recorded in fix_note).
universal_fields.kind: fix | design | perf. fix: agent can autonomously investigate and resolve. design: requires human architectural or planning decisions before a fix can be written — out of scope for the debug harness. perf: measurement-driven, requires a separate protocol. Issues are filed as fix by default; the debug harness reclassifies to design if investigation reveals the fix requires human decisions (see run_debug.md).
universal_fields.status: open | investigating | root-cause-found | needs-design | blocked | needs-manual-verification | closed. See status_semantics for meaning of each value.
universal_fields.title: string — one line, plain English
universal_fields.description: string — what is observed to be wrong and why it matters. Updated by the debug harness if investigation reveals a more precise understanding.
universal_fields.location: string? — code address: file:line or subsystem name. null when not yet pinpointed.
universal_fields.caused_by: string? — issue ID this is a direct downstream consequence of. null if independent.
universal_fields.introduced_commit: string? — full or short git SHA where this was introduced. null if unknown.
universal_fields.introduced_date: string? — ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD) when first observed or committed. null if unknown.
universal_fields.fix_note: string? — current best understanding of fix direction or design requirement. Updated by the debug harness as investigation progresses. For design issues: describes what human decision is required. null if not yet investigated.
universal_fields.investigation_file: string? — path to active investigation scratchpad, e.g. docs/debug/investigations/F001.md. Set when investigation begins; cleared (set to null) when issue is closed.
universal_fields.root_cause_file: string? — path to final root-cause narrative, e.g. docs/debug/investigations/F001-root-cause.md. Set when root cause is confirmed; never cleared.
universal_fields.blocked_question: string? — the specific question a human must answer before investigation can continue. Only non-null when status is blocked. Human recovery: answer the question in fix_note and set status back to investigating.
universal_fields.fixed_in_commit: string? — git SHA of the commit that resolved this issue. null until closed.
universal_fields.fixed_date: string? — ISO 8601 date the fix landed. null until closed.

status_semantics.open: Not yet touched by the debug harness.
status_semantics.investigating: Active or paused investigation. Scratchpad exists at investigation_file. Resumable by the harness.
status_semantics.root-cause-found: An agent (possibly from a prior run) concluded the root cause is known. This is a belief, not a verified fact. A resuming agent should read root_cause_file and verify the diagnosis before committing to fix mode. If the fix attempt fails, downgrade back to investigating. Scratchpad and root_cause_file are both populated.
status_semantics.needs-design: Investigation revealed the fix requires human architectural or planning decisions — the agent cannot autonomously resolve this. fix_note describes what the design session must decide. kind has been updated to design. Human recovery: run a brainstorming or planning session, then either set kind back to fix and status to investigating if a concrete fix emerges, or leave as design.
status_semantics.blocked: Investigation stalled: 3+ distinct hypotheses ruled out with evidence, cannot proceed without human input. blocked_question states what the human must answer. Human recovery: answer in fix_note and set status to investigating.
status_semantics.needs-manual-verification: A fix has been committed and tests pass, but the issue requires human runtime confirmation (e.g. UI/input behaviour not covered by cargo-nextest). Human recovery: if runtime check confirms fix, set status to closed. If fix did not work, set status back to investigating and clear fixed_in_commit/fixed_date.
status_semantics.closed: Resolved and verified. fixed_in_commit and fixed_date are populated.

fix.repro: string? — reproduction steps. null if not yet documented.

design.repro: string? — reproduction steps or description of the undesired behaviour that motivated filing. null if not applicable.

details.audit_source: string? — original E### ID from docs/superpowers/notes/2026-04-19-port-divergence-raw-material.json if this issue originated from the port divergence audit. null otherwise.

perf.repro: string? — reproduction steps. null if not yet documented.

details.metric: string? — what is measured, e.g. frame_time_ms, paint_throughput_mpx_per_sec. null if not yet defined.
details.observed: string? — measured value at time of filing. null if not yet measured.
details.target: string? — threshold or C++ baseline this should meet. null if not yet defined.
details.benchmark: string? — command to reproduce the measurement. null if not yet written.

issues.title: Rc<RefCell<EngineScheduler>> — crosscutting architectural divergence
issues.description: C++ emGUIFramework owns the scheduler directly; engines reach it via a raw pointer threaded through Cycle/EngineCtx. Rust uses Rc<RefCell<EngineScheduler>> introduced pre-Port-Ideology (2026-03-04), unannotated. All 12 B01 workarounds (pending queues, try_borrow guards, SchedOp variants) are downstream consequences.
issues.fix_note: Requires architectural design decision: choose among three alternatives documented in docs/superpowers/notes/2026-04-19-scheduler-refcell-workaround-ledger.md §6 — (1) event loop owns &mut EngineScheduler threaded through Cycle/EngineCtx, (2) interior sharding, (3) other. Design session required before implementation.

issues.title: try_borrow re-entrancy guard in SVPUpdSlice throttle
issues.description: C++ reads GetTimeSliceCounter() inline with no guard. Rust SVPUpdSlice::DoTimeSlice reads the scheduler's time-slice counter via scheduler.try_borrow().ok() with a cached-field fallback because plain borrow() panics re-entrantly inside DoTimeSlice. Exists only because of D001.
issues.fix_note: Resolves automatically when D001 is resolved — direct &mut access eliminates the re-entrancy. No independent design work required.

issues.title: Tabbing out and back in disables zoom
issues.description: After losing and regaining window focus (Alt+Tab), scroll-wheel zoom stops working. Zoom was functional before the focus round-trip. Suspected cause: input state or VIF state not reset/restored correctly on WindowEvent::Focused transitions.
issues.location: crates/emcore/src/emGUIFramework.rs — Focused event handler; crates/emcore/src/emView.rs — SetFocused
issues.fix_note: Not yet investigated. Need runtime instrumentation at focus-change boundary (emGUIFramework::Focused handler, emView::SetFocused, emInputState, VIF chain).
issues.repro: Launch app. Scroll-wheel zoom in/out to confirm it works. Alt+Tab away. Alt+Tab back. Attempt scroll-wheel zoom — broken.

issues.title: Zoom is choppy
issues.description: Scroll-wheel zoom feels choppy compared to the expected smooth spring animation. Likely a frame-rate or animation-step issue, but root cause not yet investigated.
issues.repro: Launch app. Scroll wheel to zoom in or out. Motion is choppy rather than smooth.
